code_matches_spec_requirements.details:
Revision 2 addresses the two critical regressions from the previous review:

1. SIGBUS in concurrent conformance tests — FIXED: Root cause was the integration test binary (tests/conformance_tests.rs) missing JIT_LOCK serialization. The fix adds JIT_LOCK.lock() at the start of run_and_capture in ALL test files, replaces cleanup_all_generators with cleanup_all_runtime_state, and joins the execution thread before dropping the backend. Additionally, the broken MPMC PoolMsg::Barrier protocol was replaced with an atomic BUSY_WORKERS counter that correctly tracks active workers regardless of crossbeam channel steal semantics.

2. test_global_registry_cross_thread_visibility — FIXED: Removed cleanup_all_generators() from individual unit tests that only need to release their own generators via mb_generator_release(). The test_cleanup_drains_registry test was updated to check per-generator removal instead of asserting the entire registry is empty (which fails under concurrent test execution).

P0/P1/P2 requirements (R1-R8, R10-R11, R13) remain correctly implemented from revision 1. R12 (iter callable sentinel) has partial implementation — named function form passes (T9.2), lambda form is blocked by pre-existing codegen crash (SIGABRT). R9 (generator send TypeError) works at runtime level but exception not catchable in generated code yet.

test_plan_has_tests.details: Spec has a ## Test Plan section. Implementation diff contains 100+ #[test] functions across multiple files. Hard reject rule does not apply.

existing_tests_pass.details:
All test suites pass deterministically in default multi-threaded mode:
- 2871 lib unit tests: PASS
- 149 conformance tests: PASS (stable across 5 consecutive runs)
- 37 generator conformance tests (7 ignored): PASS
- 9 iterator conformance tests (14 ignored): PASS
- 9 gen_thread_pool_tests: PASS
- All other test binaries (p0, runtime_bugs, behavioral_*, fixture_tests, jit_tests, no_arg_constructor): PASS

The previous SIGBUS crash and non-deterministic output corruption are fully resolved by adding JIT_LOCK to all integration test binaries.

soft_checklist.code_quality: High quality. The BUSY_WORKERS atomic counter is a clean replacement for the broken MPMC barrier. The JIT_LOCK addition to test files follows a consistent pattern. Thread joining before backend drop is a correct defense-in-depth measure.
soft_checklist.error_handling: Adequate for the scope of changes. Pre-existing error handling gaps (mb_pow_mod, mb_int_base) were noted in previous review but are out of scope for this conformance xfail reduction change.
soft_checklist.performance: JIT_LOCK serializes tests as intended. The BUSY_WORKERS spin-wait uses std::hint::spin_loop() which is minimal overhead (usually 0-1 iterations since generators are cleaned up before checking). Test execution time is ~1.1s for 149 conformance tests.
soft_checklist.documentation: Inline comments clearly explain the BUSY_WORKERS purpose, the MPMC barrier bug it fixes, and why thread joining is needed before backend drop.

summary: Revision 2 successfully fixes both critical test regressions (SIGBUS crash and non-deterministic output corruption) that caused the previous REJECTED verdict. The root cause was missing JIT_LOCK serialization in the integration test binary, which allowed concurrent JIT compilation and execution. All tests now pass deterministically in default multi-threaded mode across multiple consecutive runs.
